interaction_phrase: designated ... by the United Nations and its member states
occurrence_sentence: The year 2025 has been designated as the International Year of Quantum Science and Technology (IYQST) by the United Nations and its member states, commemorating a century since the foundational developments of quantum mechanics.

source_actor: Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India
interaction_phrase: Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India is pleased to present
occurrence_sentence: In this momentous year, the Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India is pleased to present the first edition of the International Technology Engagement Strategy - Quantum (ITES-Q) report, released on World Quantum Day, April 14, 2025.

A unique and practical feature of this report is the inclusion of detailed information on India's National Quantum Mission (NQM)—including key researchers, institutions, and thematic areas—thereby making it easier for global stakeholders to identify potential collaborators.

For the purpose of this strategy document, more focus is given to the 4 verticals of India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) namely: quantum computing, quantum communication, quantum sensing & metrology, and quantum materials & devices.

The Mission provides a huge boost to National priorities like Digital India, Make in India, Start-up India, Aatmanirbhar India and Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) and provides complementarity to national missions such as National Supercomputing Mission (NSM), National Mission on Integrated Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS).
For India, Quantum holds strategic importance in enhancing technology security & competitiveness and India's position in the global technology landscape.
NQM, launched in 2023, aims to help various sectors, including communication, health, finance, and energy with applications in drug design, space, banking, security, etc.

The Mission provides a huge boost to National priorities like Digital India, Make in India, Start-up India, Aatmanirbhar India and Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) and provides complementarity to national missions such as National Supercomputing Mission (NSM), National Mission on Integrated Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS).

With an aim to complement and strengthen the ambitions of the NQM and other ongoing efforts by various agencies and stakeholders in the ecosystem, this strategy document (ITES-Q) focuses on QSTI to enable informed and adaptive international cooperation capturing the timely opportunities and help leverage a higher degree of impact for longer-term goals.

interaction_phrase: complement and strengthen the ambitions of the NQM
occurrence_sentence: With an aim to complement and strengthen the ambitions of the NQM and other ongoing efforts by various agencies and stakeholders in the ecosystem, this strategy document (ITES-Q) focuses on QSTI to enable informed and adaptive international cooperation capturing the timely opportunities and help leverage a higher degree of impact for longer-term goals.

interaction_phrase: designated as the International Year of Quantum Science and Technology
occurrence_sentence: Furthermore, the year 2025 being designated [1] as the International Year of Quantum Science and Technology [2] by the United Nations highlights the global recognition of the importance of quantum technologies.

From a functional perspective of this report, detailed information on India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) - key researchers, institutions, and thematic areas-hubs and spokes have been included with an aim to streamline possible collaborations going forward.

interaction_phrase: to join the UK's commercial quantum secure metro network
occurrence_sentence: HSBC partnering with the University of Edinburgh and becoming the first bank [4] to join the UK's commercial quantum secure metro network and its partnership with Toshiba [5] for developing their post-quantum cryptography strategy is also an example of a foreign bank advancing in quantum.
Roche is already collaborating with Cambridge Quantum Computing to explore molecular interaction simulations using quantum algorithms in drug discovery.

Countries like the US, Germany, and the U.K. have leveraged government-backed VC funds, public-private partnerships (PPPs), and corporate-led investment consortia to derisk quantum investments and attract institutional capital.

Unlike global counterparts where deep-tech VCs play a major role, apart from government public funding, India's quantum technology ecosystem funding is primarily driven by angel investors, with limited participation from institutional investors such as venture capital (VC) firms,  tech corporates and private equity (PE) funds.

The data analysis of the four verticals of quantum on the basis of the top countries that Indian researchers are collaborating with highlights research partnerships India has cultivated with specific countries in different areas of quantum technology with varying stages of research and development.
The U.S. emerges as the top country for collaboration across all quantum verticals in both patents and publications.
The U.S. emerges as the top country for collaboration across all quantum verticals in both patents and publications.
In joint publications, the US shows particularly strong numbers in Quantum Materials and Devices (758) and Quantum Computing (92).

In Publications, Quantum Materials and Devices emerge as the most collaborative domain, with significantly higher publication numbers than other fields and broader international participation. Collaboration with South Korea leads here with 758 joint publications.

Quantum Communication & Cryptography show an equal number of partnerships in patents (UK, US, Belgium) but more distributed international collaboration in publications, with countries such as the Czech Republic, Australia and England showing significant activity.
European countries, including France and the U.K, are significant collaborators for India in both publications and patents, though their prominence varies across the verticals.

occurrence_sentence: The government, along with funding various programs in this field, is funding startups focusing on various aspects of quantum technology.

The Government of India has launched several initiatives to support the development of quantum technologies, with the National Quantum Mission (NQM) with an outlay of Rs. 6003.65 crores being the flagship initiative.

The Union Cabinet approved the National Quantum Mission (NQM) [83] in 2023 with a total budget of Rs.6003.65 for the time period of 2023-24 to 2030-31, aiming to seed, nurture and scale up scientific and industrial R&D and create a vibrant & innovative ecosystem in Quantum Technology.

The operationalisation of NQM is spearheaded by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), led by Prof. Abhay Karandikar (Secretary, DST – dstsec@nic.in) and team including that of Dr. JBV Reddy (Scientist-F, DST – jbvreddy@nic.in)
In a major step for India's National Quantum Mission (NQM), premier institutions were selected [84] to establish Thematic Hubs (T-Hubs).
In a major step for India's National Quantum Mission (NQM), premier institutions were selected to establish Thematic Hubs (T-Hubs).

interaction_phrase: US-India initiative on Critical and Emerging Technology
occurrence_sentence: US-India initiative on Critical and Emerging Technology (US-India iCET) [94], announced in May 2022, aims to elevate and expand the strategic technology partnership and defence industrial cooperation between the governments, businesses, and academic institutions of the two countries.

Within this, the US-India Quantum Coordination Mechanism is facilitating bilateral cooperation in Quantum Information Science and Technology, including workshops on post-quantum cryptography and quantum hardware, and enabling visits of Indian technical experts to U.S. quantum institutions and national laboratories.

interaction_phrase: US-India Transforming the Relationship Utilizing Strategic Technology
occurrence_sentence: U.S.-India Transforming the Relationship Utilizing Strategic Technology (US-India TRUST) [95] initiative was announced in February 2025, which aims to catalyse government-to-government, academia and private sector collaboration to promote the application of critical and emerging technologies in areas like defence, artificial intelligence, semiconductors, quantum, biotechnology, energy and space.

UK-India Technology Security Initiative [96] (UK-India TSI), launched in July 2024, builds upon the ambitious bilateral cooperation agenda set out in the India-UK Roadmap 2030 and focuses on quantum technologies as part of broader cooperation in critical and emerging technologies.
